method.target: required major preparation in one pinned ASSIST template per UC program
method.template_selection: modal raw ASSIST template among the 115 configured agreements for each selected program; a deterministic parsed representative is pinned within that raw template
method.district_pooling: A complete articulation path may come from any member college; path components are never split across colleges.
method.course_objective: Minimum distinct actual major-preparation courses, then minimum native units, jointly across every strictly reachable pinned program; known prerequisites are added afterward.
method.prerequisite_model: Known concept-matched prerequisites remain local to the college offering the selected course.
method.term_objective: Minimum regular terms for the fixed minimum-course plan under a 15-native-unit cap.
method.calendar_comparison: Academic years use two terms for semester districts and three terms for quarter districts.

warnings: Major preparation only; general education, admission, associate-degree, seat, conflict, and post-transfer requirements are outside this model.
A district plan assumes students can cross-enroll at any member college.
Term estimates assume every selected course is offered every regular term without timetable conflicts.
Known prerequisites are added after the minimum major-preparation course set is selected, so elapsed time is not optimized across every alternative course set.
